book_claims.mechanism: White fish providing modest vitamin D and omega-3
book_claims.text: Listed as a source of vitamin D (144 IU per fillet raw, 36% DV) and omega-3 (0.1 g EPA/DHA per 75 g cooked)

supplementary_research.finding: Tilapia has a high omega-6 to omega-3 ratio (11:1); consumption without balancing with high omega-3 fish may promote rather than reduce inflammation
Tilapia provides complete lean protein and vitamin D; low mercury makes it safe but low EPA/DHA means it should not replace oily fish as the primary fish source

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Lean protein; all essential amino acids; lower calorie density than oily fish
Useful dietary vitamin D source; fat-soluble, enhanced with dietary fat
Very low omega-3; compensate with sardines or anchovies in the same meal plan week

synergies.note: Tilapia is low in omega-3; pairing weeks with sardines balances omega-3 intake in varied fish rotation
Cooking tilapia in olive oil adds MUFA and improves vitamin D absorption
Neutral flavor of tilapia pairs well with bold vegetable preparations; standard Longevity Diet dinner combination
